messages.content:
Accenture has set ambitious sustainability goals such as recycling 100% of its waste and significantly reducing water usage. The company plans to achieve net zero by _______.


2025

2050

2040

2030

What is the aim of the European Union’s Sustainable Financial Disclosure Regulation?


It is aimed at obtaining transparent, traceable, and quality environmental, social, and governance (ESG) data from companies across their value chains.

It is aimed at ensuring that a company has a strong environmental agenda and becomes an environmentally responsible organization.

It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

It is aimed at ensuring transparency in the sourcing of raw materials and managing relationships in the value chain.

It is aimed at ensuring asset managers, financial advisors, and other financial market participants consider sustainability and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in their decision-making around investments.

Intelligent solutions through digital transformation drive results from every aspect of the responsible value chain. Which of the following is an example of how intelligent solutions drive impact at the design stage?


Digitizing the research and development processes

Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint

Introducing product platform engineering and modernization
Leveraging the digital twin technology to help clients optimize design to build products with low carbon footprint.

Identify the five important elements that businesses need to examine in any ambition-setting exercise.


Capabilities; governance; operating models; systems; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; processes; technology, tools, and insights; roles and responsibilities; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives

Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; operating models; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives
Capabilities; governance; technology, tools, and insights; operating models; key performance indicators (KPIs) and incentives.

Match each key dimension of Circular against its appropriate action.


1.
Use

 
Involves planning and development of a circular product or service

2.
End-of-use

 
Involves enabling multiple uses of a product or service

3.
Design

 
Involves maximizing value by eliminating waste

4.
Use extension

 
Involves maximizing the value delivered from a single use of a product or service through a sharing platform model

1. Design
Involves planning and development of a circular product or service

2. Use extension
Involves enabling multiple uses of a product or service

3. Use
Involves maximizing the value delivered from a single use of a product or service through a sharing platform model

4. End-of-use
Involves maximizing value by eliminating waste

A leading consumer goods firm has adopted Accenture’s 360° Value framework. A responsible value chain is a significant component of this framework. How can this contribute to the consumer experience and growth of their brand?


A responsible value chain will help the company to establish responsible production and consumption practices throughout its manufacturing processes.

A responsible value chain will help the company to provide complete transparency to their consumers on the ecological and ethical footprint of the company’s products and services.

A responsible value chain will enable the company to reduce its carbon footprint and improve its social responsibility measures.
A responsible value chain will help the company to provide complete transparency to their consumers on the ecological and ethical footprint of the company’s products and services.

How can a multinational retail chain embed a responsibility mindset across its product and operations value chain through its vision and strategy?


By following resource-optimized sourcing or a renewable energy sourcing strategy

By working closely with the supplier ecosystem in its value chain to set goals for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and provide incentives and credits for sustainable initiatives

By designing responsible and intelligent products and platforms

By fleet electrification or route optimization in its logistics functions
By working closely with the supplier ecosystem in its value chain to set goals for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and provide incentives and credits for sustainable initiatives.

Identify the key drivers that enable businesses to win stakeholder trust in a responsible value chain.


Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption

Ethical sourcing of raw materials and supply chain traceability

Fair wages and health and safety for all workers

Responsible treatment of hazardous substances and supply chain traceability
Supply chain traceability, supplier ethics and transparency, working conditions including fair wages and health and safety, and zero corruption.

Aercrux Corporation Pvt. Ltd. has decided to embrace the opportunities of a responsible value chain along the three pillars of Trust, Net Zero, and Circular. Which of the following actions can the company take toward achieving Net Zero?


Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Provide a living wage or income to everyone who directly provides goods and services to the company.

Make their materials packaging returnable or recyclable.
Transition to 100% renewable electricity in production and become carbon negative for all their direct and indirect emissions.

Match each sustainability performance pillar with its appropriate indicators


1.
Environmental

 
Percentage reduction in carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions and water consumption at plants and distribution centers, percentage of products with an improved environmental or social profile

2.
Social

 
Percentage of permanent employees with access to healthcare coverage, percentage of employees benefitting from at least one training session per year

3.
Governance

 
Number of bribery and corruption incidents, reports of business ethics breaches, percentage of gender diversity in senior management

1. Environmental
Percentage reduction in carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions and water consumption at plants and distribution centers, percentage of products with an improved environmental or social profile

2. Social
Percentage of permanent employees with access to healthcare coverage, percentage of employees benefitting from at least one training session per year

3. Governance
Number of bribery and corruption incidents, reports of business ethics breaches, percentage of gender diversity in senior management

Which of the following is not the responsibility of the chief financial officer (CFO) in an organization’s sustainability measurement activities?


Aligning non-financial aspects of performance with the financial corporate strategy

Building innovative business cases to demonstrate the value of sustainability

Measuring only the financial growth of the organization
Measuring only the financial growth of the organization.

Under the Non-Financial Reporting Directive (NFRD) from the European Union (EU), what are large public-interest companies required to disclose?


Reveal how they operate and address social and environmental challenges.

Reveal the financial data of their operations in Europe.

Disclose the steps they take to upskill their employees on ethics and compliance issues.
Reveal how they operate and address social and environmental challenges.

What does the “economy of care” focus on?


The increasing rate of population growth and the subsequent need for greater resources

The importance of qualitative key performance indicators (KPIs)

The business strategies adopted by various companies to upskill employees
The importance of qualitative key performance indicators (KPIs).

Which of the following is a European Union (EU)-wide classification system for sustainable activities?


EU Taxonomy

Green New Deal

Paris Agreement
EU Taxonomy.

Which of the following is not a consequence for businesses that fail to integrate sustainability measurement in their operations?


They could be penalized in terms of poor environmental, social, and governance (ESG) goals.

They may lose out on critical growth opportunities.

They will be able to respond to market changes in a more agile manner.
They will be able to respond to market changes in a more agile manner.

Budget updates, revising planning for specific business units, and establishing incentives for achieving sustainability goals are examples of actions for which key area of sustainability performance measurement?


Analyze

Adapt

Identify

Aggregate
Adapt.

Which measuring technique is useful in benchmarking a company’s environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting with the leading frameworks on sustainability and ESG disclosures and reporting?


Sustainability Gap analysis

Materiality score

Crisis management communication plan

Sustainability roadmap
Sustainability Gap analysis.

How can artificial intelligence (AI), big data, machine learning (ML), and data analytics facilitate better and faster business decisions?


By enabling better coverage and integration of financial data

By providing companies with mature data and enhanced reporting capabilities

By filtering out non-verifiable data that can disrupt the decision-making process
By providing companies with mature data and enhanced reporting capabilities.

Onboarding and training middle management on new reporting processes and key performance indicators (KPIs) is a core component of a company’s _______.


change management strategy

operating model

sustainability framework

governance framework
change management strategy.

_______ is Accenture’s custom-built, comprehensive, and transparent cloud platform that offers quick access to reliable and environmental, social, and governance (ESG)-pertinent data lake solutions.


CFO ESG Maturity Matrix

ESG 360

Sustainability performance measurement (SPM) solution
ESG 360.

Match each sustainable technology with its appropriate description.


1.
Sustainable software

 
Looks at the environmental and social impacts of development of new digital technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and blockchain

2.
Sustainable cloud

 
Focuses on data centers, devices, data, and applications as the main levers for decarbonization

3.
Sustainable IT

 
Allows organizations to tap on-demand public, private, or hybrid computing capabilities using virtual hardware, software, and services

1. Sustainable software
Looks at the environmental and social impacts of development of new digital technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and blockchain

2. Sustainable cloud
Focuses on data centers, devices, data, and applications as the main levers for decarbonization

3. Sustainable IT
Allows organizations to tap on-demand public, private, or hybrid computing capabilities using virtual hardware, software, and services.

A pharmaceutical firm wants to transition to sustainable technologies. As part of this goal, they want to move their applications from enterprise-owned servers to the cloud. What sustainable benefit will they achieve by this migration?


Reduced server maintenance effort

Greater access to finance

Reduced carbon emissions
Reduced carbon emissions.

Which of the following is not a benefit of using sustainable technologies?


Increased transparency of supply chains

Greater virtual collaboration

Reduced cost of operations

Reduced operational IT footprint
Reduced operational IT footprint.

Which of the following is an example of a societal benefit of using sustainable technologies?


Increased transparency in supply chains

Reuse of devices using the sharing platform model

Use of renewable technologies in operations
Reuse of devices using the sharing platform model.

Which of the following should not be considered by business leaders while thinking about achieving their sustainability goals?


Increasing the complexity of daily operations

Selecting a carbon-thoughtful provider

Unlocking financial, societal, and environmental benefits

Shifting enterprise-owned computing to the cloud
Increasing the complexity of daily operations.

Which of the following is not a key dimension of 360 Value that companies should focus on to achieve their sustainability goals?


Operational efficiency

Financial

Talent

Diversity & Inclusion
Financial.

Fill in the blanks: Improved accessibility to data and information, _____________, and greater circularity of hardware and servers are desired customer outcomes of combining sustainable cloud, IT, and software.


improved products or services

responsible management of data and applications

increased customer touchpoints
improved products or services.

How can sustainable technologies help meet sustainability goals and deliver significant business value?


Reduce the operational footprint of technology itself and enable the broader business, stakeholders, and ecosystem to reduce their footprint

Collaborate with strategic partners to support market demand.

Help organizations set strong ethical standards so they can operate responsibly in everything they do
Reduce the operational footprint of technology itself and enable the broader business, stakeholders, and ecosystem to reduce their footprint.

Place each step for setting and meeting ambitions for sustainable technologies in the correct order.


 
Start with the big picture of the corporate sustainability agenda.

 
Align the technology-specific agenda to overall sustainability goals.

 
Recognize the IT maturity level and focus on strategic priorities.

 
Translate ambition to action for desired outcomes.

 
Deploy intelligently, and track and measure over time.

 
Use sustainable technologies to support sustainability goals.

1. Start with the big picture of the corporate sustainability agenda.

2. Align the technology-specific agenda to overall sustainability goals.

3. Recognize the IT maturity level and focus on strategic priorities.

4. Use sustainable technologies to support sustainability goals.

5. Translate ambition to action for desired outcomes.

6. Deploy intelligently, and track and measure over time.

Place each step for setting and meeting ambitions for sustainable technologies in the correct order.


Step 2	
 
Start with the big picture of the corporate sustainability agenda.

Step 4	
 
Align the technology-specific agenda to overall sustainability goals.

Step 6	
 
Recognize the IT maturity level and focus on strategic priorities.

Step 3	
 
Translate ambition to action for desired outcomes.

Step 1	
 
Deploy intelligently, and track and measure over time.

Step 5	
 
Use sustainable technologies to support sustainability goals.

1. Start with the big picture of the corporate sustainability agenda.

2. Align the technology-specific agenda to overall sustainability goals.

3. Recognize the IT maturity level and focus on strategic priorities.

4. Translate ambition to action for desired outcomes.

5. Deploy intelligently, and track and measure over time.

6. Use sustainable technologies to support sustainability goals.

Which of the following is not a feature of virtualization of servers?


Enables creation of multiple simulated environments from a single, physical hardware system

Scales server capacity on demand

Conserves energy in enterprise data centers

Separates applications at the operating-system level
Requires physical hardware for each individual environment.

Which of the following is not a feature of virtualization of servers?


Enables creation of multiple simulated environments from a single, physical hardware system

Scales server capacity on demand

Conserves energy in enterprise data centers

Separates applications at the operating-system level
Requires physical hardware for each individual environment.

Which of the following is not a feature of virtualization of servers?


Enables creation of multiple simulated environments from a single, physical hardware system

Scales server capacity on demand

Conserves energy in enterprise data centers

Separates applications at the operating-system level
Requires physical hardware for each individual environment.

Why must businesses embrace breakthrough technology, such as artificial intelligence (AI) and green cloud, to take action on climate change?


Technology can help businesses quickly transition to renewable sources of energy.

Technology can help businesses not only reduce their carbon emissions but also their cost of operations.

Technology can help businesses make their value chains agile in a volatile market
Technology can help businesses not only reduce their carbon emissions but also their cost of operations.

A leading ice-cream brand has around 2 million freezer cabinets across different countries. The refrigerants such as hydrofluorocarbon (HFC), along with the high amounts of energy, used to keep the freezers cold have a significant carbon footprint. What measures can the company take to reduce the carbon impact caused by its freezers?


Replace HFC freezers with those containing natural refrigerants such as hydrocarbons with low carbon impact. Also, use renewable energy sources to directly power the freezers.

Switch off freezers at regular intervals so that there is less energy consumption.

Ask the company’s retailers to own and operate the freezers so that the onus of using HFC freezers is not on the ice-cream brand.
Replace HFC freezers with those containing natural refrigerants such as hydrocarbons with low carbon impact. Also, use renewable energy sources to directly power the freezers.

You are part of a team that’s researching the carbon intensity of Accenture’s supplier companies. The team is calculating the existing carbon emissions of these companies and finding ways to reduce the emissions. Which of Accenture’s sustainability growth priorities are you addressing through this project?


Consumer brand, growth, and experience

Sustainable technologies

Responsible value chains

Sustainability measurement
Responsible value chains.

As a citizen, consumer, and employee, Oliver believes in playing his part to address climate change. He votes for governments that are committed to the Paris Agreement, shops responsibly, and engages with colleagues on sustainability innovations. As members of society, just like Oliver, why is it critical for us to step up and facilitate action on climate change?


Numerous feedback loops in our society aggravate climate change, making the Earth warmer.

We have already exhausted our carbon budget, causing irreparable damage to our planet.

The Paris Agreement mandates each of us to take action on climate change by adopting sustainable practices such as switching to renewable energy sources.
Numerous feedback loops in our society aggravate climate change, making the Earth warmer.

Accenture’s climate leadership strategy comprises four pillars. Match each pillar with the appropriate description.

A. Setting science-based, industry-leading Net Zero and other ambitious sustainability goals B. Working with employees to ensure a greener future C. Involving suppliers in action on climate change D. Partnering with clients and ecosystem partners


Leading by example; Magnifying impact; Empowering eco-action; Innovating

Leading by example; Magnifying impact; Innovating; Empowering eco-action

Leading by example; Empowering eco-action; Magnifying impact; Innovating

Empowering eco-action; Magnifying impact; Innovating; Leading by example
